mnemonics: CRUFT: CPU Utilization, Response time, Throughput, Fairness, Turnaround time
keyPoints: CPU Utilization: Keeping the CPU as busy as possible.
Throughput: Number of processes completed per time unit.
Turnaround Time: Total time from submission to completion (includes waiting and execution).
Waiting Time: Amount of time a process has been waiting in the ready queue.
Response Time: Time from submission until the first response is produced (important for interactive systems).
commonMistakes: Confusing Turnaround Time (completion) with Response Time (first output).
Assuming a higher Throughput always means better performance, without considering Response Time for interactive users.
Over-optimizing for one criterion (e.g., CPU Utilization) and severely degrading another (e.g., Response Time).
